event: The Virgin nursing the Christ Child
symbols: nursing infant — the Incarnation in its most intimate aspect, God nourished by human flesh
Mary's red robe — the blood of the Passion foreshadowed even in the scene of nursing
landscape visible through window — the world into which the Christ Child has been born
tender embrace — the depth of Mary's maternal love for the divine Son
description: Within an ornate gilded frame, the Virgin Mary sits holding the Christ Child who nurses at her breast. Mary wears a red robe and has her hair partially covered with a dark cloth. Her expression is gentle and contemplative as she gazes down at the infant. The child is nude and looks upward. Behind them, through an opening or window, a mountainous landscape with blue peaks and green valleys is visible. The painting combines Flemish realism in the figures with an Italianate landscape background.
theological_description: The Maria Lactans (nursing Madonna) is among the most theologically profound of Marian images, for it confronts the viewer with the full reality of the Incarnation. The Creator of the universe, through whom all things were made (JHN.1.3), here depends entirely on a human mother for sustenance. This is not a metaphor — it is the literal implication of the Incarnation: 'the Word became flesh' (JHN.1.14) means that God truly became a nursing infant. The Church Fathers recognized in this image the reversal of Eve's curse: where Eve's eating brought death, Mary's nursing of the New Adam brings life. Augustine writes that the breasts that nursed Christ are more blessed than the womb that bore Him — yet both point to the same mystery of divine condescension. Mary's contemplative gaze reflects her characteristic response of pondering these things in her heart (LUK.2.19), as the fullness of what she holds gradually becomes clear.

scripture_refs.reason: Mary giving birth and caring for the infant Jesus — the immediate context of the nursing scene
The Word became flesh — the radical reality of the Incarnation visible in God being nursed
Mary treasuring all these things and pondering them in her heart — her contemplative gaze
Blessed is the womb that bore you and the breasts at which you nursed
